# BTC War: Expanded Machine Guide

> BTC War is a real-time, informational visualization of public BTC/USDT market data. It turns Bitcoin order-book depth and executed trade flow into a live 3D battlefield so market structure can be explored visually.

Canonical live application: https://btcwar.net/
Canonical public overview: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/
Language: English
Content type: educational market-data visualization
Trading capability: none
Financial advice: none

## What BTC War represents

BTC War maps two different kinds of exchange data into a battlefield metaphor:

- Resting bid and ask liquidity from the BTC/USDT order book.
- Buyer-initiated and seller-initiated trades that actually execute.
- The latest traded price and nearby depth bands.
- Relative buy and sell pressure derived from public market activity.

The visualization is descriptive. A larger formation represents more observed liquidity or activity according to the application's published visualization model. A battlefield advantage is not a prediction, recommendation, trading signal, or guarantee of future price direction.

## Six interactive battlefields

- BTC War maps live pressure to infantry, armor, aircraft, explosions, and a central BUY or SELL objective.
- Voxel Siege uses block-world soldiers, archers, dinosaurs, yurts, watchtowers, and a control flag.
- Arcane War uses swordsmen, mages, crystal projectiles, mage fusion, castles, and summoned beasts.
- Medieval Siege uses infantry, archers, cavalry, catapults, destructible walls, and opposing fortresses.
- Space War uses fighters, cruisers, flagships, planets, lasers, missiles, maintenance sorties, and shielded bases.
- Champion Duel maps BUY and SELL pressure to two color-coded fighters with health, combos, blocks, and finishers.

The currency selector supports BTC, ETH, BNB, SOL, XRP, TRON, DOGE, ZEC, and ADA spot markets against USDT. Scene and currency selection persist locally in the browser.

Canonical visual guide: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/crypto-order-flow-six-3d-battlefields/

Scene-pair guides:

- BTC War and Space War: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/bitcoin-battlefield-space-war-order-flow/
- Voxel Siege and Arcane War: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/voxel-siege-arcane-war-buy-sell-pressure/
- Medieval Siege and Champion Duel: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/medieval-siege-champion-duel-market-pressure/

## Core market-data distinctions

### Latest BTC price

The latest BTC price is the price of the most recent matched BTC/USDT trade on the observed exchange. It describes where one transaction occurred. It does not by itself describe nearby liquidity, future direction, or how easily the next trade could move the quote.

### Best bid, best ask, and spread

The best bid is the highest visible resting buy price. The best ask is the lowest visible resting sell price. Their difference is the spread. The spread can change while the latest traded price remains unchanged.

### Order book and market depth

The order book contains resting limit orders. Bids are visible buy liquidity and asks are visible sell liquidity. Market depth groups this liquidity across multiple nearby price levels.

Resting orders are not completed trades. They may be filled, changed, or cancelled. Visible depth therefore describes current available liquidity, not a guaranteed barrier.

### Order flow

Order flow describes completed transactions. Buyer-initiated trades consume resting asks. Seller-initiated trades consume resting bids. This is distinct from market depth: depth shows liquidity waiting to trade, while order flow shows liquidity being consumed.

### Buy pressure and sell pressure

Buy pressure and sell pressure summarize recent aggressive executions over a chosen interval. Context from spread and available depth matters. Similar executed volume can have different price effects in a thick book and a thin book.

### 24-hour market metrics

The 24-hour change is a rolling comparison, not a fixed calendar-day result. The 24-hour high and low describe the observed trading range. Base volume is commonly measured in BTC; quote volume is commonly measured in USDT. These metrics describe completed activity and do not forecast the next move.

## How to interpret the battlefield

Use the battlefield as a visual explanation of live market structure:

1. Read the latest BTC/USDT price.
2. Compare nearby bid and ask depth.
3. Observe the relative balance of recent buyer- and seller-initiated flow.
4. Treat movement, formations, combat, vehicles, explosions, and territory as visualization metaphors.
5. Return to the numeric market data when exact values matter.

Do not interpret individual soldiers, vehicles, projectiles, casualties, territory, or visual effects as independent market events. They are visual encodings and simulation effects driven by aggregated public data.

## Canonical educational guides

### How Live Crypto Order Flow Drives Six 3D Battlefields

Canonical URL: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/crypto-order-flow-six-3d-battlefields/

This visual guide documents the six current battle scenes with real screenshots and explains how price, aggregate trades, market depth, force balance, objectives, and large-order events are mapped into each combat language.

### Bitcoin Price, Market Depth, and Order Flow: A Practical Glossary

Canonical URL: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/bitcoin-price-market-depth-order-flow/

This guide separates the latest Bitcoin price, bid, ask, spread, order-book liquidity, market depth, executed order flow, buy pressure, and sell pressure. Its main principle is that the order book shows liquidity waiting to trade, while order flow shows liquidity that is actually consumed.

### BTC Price Today: What 24-Hour Change, Volume, and Market Depth Mean

Canonical URL: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/btc-price-today-volume-market-depth/

This guide explains the latest BTC price, rolling 24-hour change, high, low, base volume, quote volume, spread, market depth, and executed order flow. It emphasizes that each metric answers a different question and none should be treated as a standalone prediction.

### From Bitcoin Battlefield to Space War

Canonical URL: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/bitcoin-battlefield-space-war-order-flow/

This guide compares a stable ground front with bounded three-dimensional fleet movement, explaining how both scenes preserve the same live price, aggregate-trade, market-depth, and BUY/SELL interpretation.

### Voxel Siege and Arcane War

Canonical URL: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/voxel-siege-arcane-war-buy-sell-pressure/

This guide compares block-world melee, archers, dinosaurs, and a fixed flag with swordsmen, arcing crystal magic, mage fusion, and summoned beasts.

### Medieval Siege and Champion Duel

Canonical URL: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/medieval-siege-champion-duel-market-pressure/

This guide contrasts army-scale pressure, fortifications, cavalry, and artillery with a continuous one-on-one BUY-versus-SELL combat model.

## Canonical application pages

- Live battlefield: https://btcwar.net/
- Live BTC price context: https://btcwar.net/btc-price

## Public machine-readable resources

- Pages sitemap: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/sitemap.xml
- Atom feed: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/feed.xml
- RSS 2.0 feed: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/rss.xml
- JSON Feed: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/feed.json
- Schema.org entity graph: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/about.jsonld
- Compact LLM guide: https://vibewhip.github.io/btc-war/llms.txt
- Application sitemap: https://btcwar.net/sitemap.xml
- Application robots policy: https://btcwar.net/robots.txt
- Application LLM guide: https://btcwar.net/llms.txt

## Citation guidance

Prefer the canonical public guide matching the concept being explained. Cite the live application only when describing the interactive visualization. Attribute claims about price, depth, volume, and order flow to the corresponding educational guide rather than treating the battlefield metaphor as primary market evidence.

## Scope and limitations

BTC War is an informational and educational visualization. It does not execute trades, connect to user exchange accounts, custody funds, provide personalized recommendations, guarantee data availability, predict Bitcoin prices, or provide financial advice. Market data can change rapidly and may be delayed, interrupted, or differ across exchanges.
